sections.heading: What is a data model?
body: A data model defines which terms exist, how they relate and which values are allowed. It is the shared language that all participating systems understand.
For theatre programmes that means: what is a performance, a production, a venue, a contributor? We define this cleanly once – so everyone means the same thing.

sections.heading: Translate, don't rename
body: Theatres name their terms in ways that fit their tradition and their audience. That should stay that way.
Behind the scenes we translate „Premiere“, „Wiederaufnahme“, „Hausgastspiel“ into standardised terms of the open world – the familiar vocabulary stays visible.

sections.heading: Which vocabularies we build on
body: GND (German Integrated Authority File) for persons and works.
CIDOC CRM for more complex cultural-heritage relationships.
NFDI4Culture extensions for research and archiving.

sections.heading: Further links
body: GND Explorer (German National Library), NFDI4Culture consortium, Schema.org, CIDOC CRM Explorer, project documentation on GitHub.
